dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: Sabre Corp — global travel-technology / GDS air-distribution + IT software.
dossiers.thesis: The equity is a thin sliver on a ~$4.6B enterprise where a real operational turn is now flowing against a shrinking-but-heavy debt load: six consecutive improving EPS prints, EBITDA and bookings inflecting while the stock is still priced like distressed debt-heavy equity ~40% below its prior high. Consensus underweights how EV re-rating + paydown compounds through ~5.5x leverage into the equity over 6-24 months.
dossiers.inflection_evidence: Q1 2026 (8-K acc 0001628280-26-031703): revenue $760M +8% YoY, Normalized Adj EBITDA $169M +21% (margin +235bps to 22.2%), air bookings +6% to 87M (2-yr high), EPS +$0.06 vs -$0.03 est (6th improving print); gross debt $5.2B(24)->$4.44B(Mar26).
dossiers.catalyst: FCF crossing positive in 2027 + continued EBITDA beats driving an EV/EBITDA re-rate through ~5.5x leverage; next print Q2 2026
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: Normalized Adj EBITDA grows YoY for the next 3 prints and gross debt falls further through FY2026.
dossiers.kill_condition: Adj EBITDA growth stalls/reverses on any print, or a debt maturity/refi shock
dossiers.adversarial: The interest burden IS the thesis: ~$450M+/yr interest (2025 refis at 11.125%) vs ~$585M EBITDA leaves razor-thin equity FCF; any EBITDA stall or GDS secular disintermediation (airline NDC/direct-connect bypass) impairs the levered equity fast and asymmetrically. Negative book equity today; can go to zero.
dossiers.blowup_note: $665M cash, >90% debt pushed to 2029+, no maturity wall; FY26 FCF ~breakeven ex-restructuring. Survives the hold.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: ACV Auctions — digital wholesale used-vehicle auction marketplace + data/inspection services.
dossiers.thesis: A washed-out net-cash marketplace where the real inflection is MARGIN, not volume: operating leverage + services monetization more than doubled adj EBITDA while the stock de-rated twice on volume guide-downs to ~1.35x EV/sales. Consensus prices a soft used-vehicle cycle as permanent; a volume reacceleration + continued Manheim/ADESA share gains re-rates EV/sales over 6-24mo. It sits LOW in its 52wk range (~26% up, -56% from high) while every arrived peer sits high in theirs.
dossiers.inflection_evidence: Adj EBITDA $59M FY2025 (>2x YoY on $760M rev +19%), guided $73-77M FY2026; Q1-26 (8-K acc 0001637873-26-000019) adj EBITDA $17M above guide, non-GAAP net income turned POSITIVE (+$7M), GAAP loss narrowing to -$11M — on units +3% / GMV +5% (operating leverage, not a volume S-curve).
dossiers.catalyst: A quarter where unit/GMV growth reaccelerates alongside another EBITDA beat, re-rating EV/S from ~1.35x toward ~2.5x; next print early Aug 2026
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: Adj EBITDA grows YoY each of the next 3 prints AND Marketplace units re-accelerate above +5% YoY within the hold.
dossiers.kill_condition: Two consecutive prints of decelerating units AND a cut to EBITDA guide
dossiers.kill_value: units decel + EBITDA guide cut x2
dossiers.adversarial: Volume growth has STALLED (units +3%, GMV +5%) on a macro used-vehicle cycle outside the company control; the market already de-rated twice on light guides (Nov-25 -40%, Feb-26 -25%). Another light guide retests the $4.50-5 lows — this is a margin-in-a-soft-cycle story, not the high-growth adoption S-curve the multiple needs.
dossiers.blowup_note: Net cash ~$141M ($341M cash vs $200M debt), positive adj EBITDA + non-GAAP NI — self-funding.
